meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging Your App: Analytics Setup for AI Builders
content.seo_description: Stop guessing why your code fails. Learn to implement strategic analytics to debug your AI apps using Antigravity and Claude Code in this intermediate guide.
content.answer_block: Debugging fails because you lack visibility into runtime state. By integrating event-based analytics, you transform 'black box' crashes into actionable data streams. This tutorial guides you through instrumenting your application to capture telemetry, allowing you to trace errors, identify bottlenecks, and maintain system health like a professional lead engineer.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks architecting your masterpiece, but it’s failing in production. The terminal is silent, the logs are empty, and you’re staring at a screen of phantom bugs that refuse to surface. This is the 'debugging wall'—the defining moment where most student projects die. You aren't failing because you're a bad coder; you're failing because you're flying blind without telemetry.

The secret that separates senior engineers from juniors isn't intuition; it's observability. When you instrument your code with intelligent analytics, you stop guessing and start diagnosing. Today, we’re bridging the gap between 'it doesn't work' and 'I know exactly why' by deploying a robust analytics layer that turns your app into a transparent, debuggable ecosystem.

steps.title: Define Your Critical Path Events
steps.description: Identify the core user actions that trigger your AI logic to ensure you track state transitions, not just page views.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and identify the 5 most critical state-changing functions that require telemetry for debugging purposes.

steps.title: Instrument the Telemetry Layer
steps.description: Inject structured logging wrappers around your AI API calls to capture input tokens, output latency, and error codes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable TypeScript logger class that captures function execution time, input arguments, and error stacks for our analytics provider.

steps.title: Automate Error Tracing
steps.description: Hook into global error handlers to automatically push stack traces to your analytics dashboard whenever a crash occurs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the global error handler in index.ts to capture and report the full execution context to the analytics service before throwing.

steps.title: Validate Data Streams
steps.description: Verify that your analytics events are firing correctly in the dev environment without polluting production data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test suite that simulates user interaction and validates that the analytics events contain the expected payload structure.

steps.title: Deploy and Monitor
steps.description: Push your changes and use the dashboard to establish a baseline of 'normal' behavior, making future anomalies instantly obvious.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a summary report of the new telemetry coverage and provide a checklist for monitoring these metrics during the first 24 hours of deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit --message='feat: add telemetry and analytics instrumentation'

pro_tips: Always log the model temperature and system prompt versions in your AI analytics to debug inconsistent outputs.
Use correlation IDs to link frontend user sessions with backend API logs; it saves hours of cross-referencing.
Implement 'sampling' on high-traffic events to keep your analytics costs low while maintaining statistical significance.
Never log raw PII (Personally Identifiable Information) in your telemetry; sanitize inputs at the middleware level.
Set up automated alerts for 'error spikes' rather than individual errors to avoid notification fatigue.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, focus on building an 'Error Dashboard' using your incoming data to visualize your app's performance trends over time.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the architecture to conquer any bug that dares cross your path. Your journey from student to AI engineer is defined by the tools you master today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
